Title: A novel transcription factor OsMYB73 affects grain size and chalkiness by regulating endosperm storage substances’ accumulation-mediated auxin biosynthesis signalling pathway in rice

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed in endosperm at 5 DAF; expression significantly decreased in cr-myb73-46
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; Y1H promoter binding; Y2H interaction; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; single- and double-mutant phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions downstream of OsMYB73 in lipid accumulation and grain chalkiness; mutant shows chalky endosperm and myb73 + ltpl36 double mutant shows belly chalky endosperm with longer grains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsLTPL36 transcript is reduced in osmyb73 mutant. 2. OsMYB73 binds the OsLTPL36 promoter and activates reporter expression. 3. OsLTPL36 protein interacts with OsMYB73 in Y2H. 4. ltpl36 mutant and myb73 + ltpl36 double mutant support a role in lipid-linked endosperm quality.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage lipid accumulation contributing to endosperm development and grain quality
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced function associates with chalkiness and likely reduced fatty acid accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for improving endosperm lipid deposition and transparency, but perturbation may induce chalkiness
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because it acts as lipid-transfer/storage-associated factor rather than enzyme or TF.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly co-expressed in endosperm at 5 DAF; targeted by OsMYB73 in developing seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H promoter binding; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; single- and double-mutant phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts downstream of OsMYB73 in amylopectin biosynthesis/endosperm development; osisa2 single mutant was transparent, but myb73 + isa2 double mutant showed more severe chalky endosperm with longer grains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB73 binds the OsISA2 promoter and activates reporter expression. 2. OsISA2 and OsMYB73 show similar high expression in 5-DAF endosperm. 3. osisa2 single mutant lacks strong chalkiness, but double mutation with osmyb73 strongly enhances chalky phenotype, indicating genetic interaction in starch organization.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Amylopectin biosynthesis contributing to normal compound starch granule formation and grain quality
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation in combination with osmyb73 aggravates chalkiness
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful node for engineering amylopectin structure, but likely requires careful modulation rather than strong disruption
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene because this paper treats it mainly as a downstream interacting/target gene rather than the primary enzyme subject, despite known enzymatic role.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Promoter targeted by OsMYB73; auxin biosynthesis-related genes broadly altered in cr-myb73-46
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H interaction; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA promoter binding context
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Proposed downstream component of OsMYB73/OsNF-YB1 module affecting auxin biosynthesis, grain filling and chalkiness
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB73 interacts with OsYUC11 and binds/activates the OsYUC11 promoter in reporter/EMSA assays. 2. Study links OsMYB73 to auxin-biosynthesis signaling during endosperm development. 3. Authors propose OsMYB73-OsNF-YB1 control of OsYUC11 contributes to storage-substance accumulation and grain chalkiness.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Auxin supports seed size, endosperm development and grain filling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation of the module is associated with chalkiness and altered grain size
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for tuning endosperm auxin to improve filling, but caution due to quality/yield trade-offs
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common Gene here because the paper experimentally treats it mainly as a downstream interactor/target within the OsMYB73 module rather than as an independently characterized pathway enzyme.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB family transcription factor with two SANT domains
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Preferentially expressed in early developing pericarp and endosperm; highest at 5 DAF; very low in root, stem, leaf, leaf sheath, and young panicle
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Biosynthesis and accumulation of endosperm storage substances; auxin biosynthesis-associated grain filling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; protein interaction-assisted transcriptional regulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsISA2; OsLTPL36; OsYUC11; OsNF-YB1 promoter binding reported
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds promoters of OsISA2, OsLTPL36, OsYUC11 and OsNF-YB1-associated motifs; mutation alters expression of starch, lipid-transfer, grain-size and auxin-biosynthesis genes; proposed to activate OsISA2, OsLTPL36 and OsYUC11
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with OsNF-YB1; proposed synergistic transcriptional activator module
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; overexpression; qRT-PCR; GUS; subcellular localization; RNA-seq; metabolomics; Y1H; Y2H; BiFC; LCI; dual-luciferase assay; EMSA; double-mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of function increases grain length and causes white-belly chalky endosperm, altered starch physicochemical properties, reduced soluble sugar and crude lipid content, increased amylose and protein; overexpression causes smaller chalky grains and reduced seed setting, 1000-grain weight and yield
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key endosperm-expressed regulator balancing storage substance accumulation and auxin-related grain filling; both deficiency and overexpression disrupt grain transparency and quality
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsMYB73 is a nucleus-localized MYB TF highly expressed in 5-DAF developing endosperm/pericarp. 2. CRISPR knockout causes longer grains and obvious chalkiness with disrupted starch granule/amyloplast organization. 3. Knockout changes starch, lipid, soluble sugar, protein and broad secondary metabolite profiles. 4. OsMYB73 binds/promotes OsISA2, OsLTPL36 and OsYUC11-related promoters and interacts with OsNF-YB1. 5. Overexpression also causes chalkiness, indicating dosage-sensitive control of grain quality.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown plants under natural conditions; developing seeds analyzed mainly at 5 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Storage reserve accumulation for grain filling and grain quality; auxin supports endosperm development and seed size
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout improves grain length but increases chalkiness and reduces grain filling rate and fresh weight; overexpression causes smaller grains, lower seed setting, lower 1000-grain weight and yield
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pOsMYB73:GUS (native promoter for expression assay); overexpression promoter NA; proOsNF-YB1::Luc; proOsISA2::Luc; proOsLTPL36::Luc; proOsYUC11::Luc in transactivation assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, metabolomics, ChIP-seq; accession number NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for balancing grain size and quality, but strong caution because both knockout and overexpression cause chalkiness; subtle allelic/promoter editing may be preferable
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors propose OsMYB73 and OsNF-YB1 act synergistically as transcription activators. ChIP-seq did not recover the same motifs found by EMSA/Y1H for key targets, indicating motif/pathway complexity.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Co-expressed in endosperm at 5 DAF according to study discussion/figure support
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Transcriptional regulation of grain filling, starch/auxin-associated endosperm development
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation in cooperation with partner TFs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: OsYUC11; OsISA2; OsLTPL36; wx and OsAGPL2 mentioned from cited context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Proposed to synergize with OsMYB73 in activation of OsISA2, OsLTPL36 and OsYUC11; prior literature cited for OsYUC11 activation
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with OsMYB73; discussion notes NF-Y complexes/heterotrimers with OsNF-YC12, OsbHLH144, OsMADS14 in other studies
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; BiFC; LCI; double-mutant phenotyping; EMSA/luciferase context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: osnf-yb1 mutant showed chalky endosperm; myb73 + nf-yb1 double mutant displayed more severe chalkiness, longer grains and strongly reduced 1000-grain weight
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important cooperative regulator with OsMYB73 for endosperm development and storage accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OsNF-YB1 physically interacts with OsMYB73. 2. Single mutant shows chalky endosperm phenotype. 3. Double mutation with osmyb73 exacerbates chalkiness and reduces grain weight, supporting synergistic function in storage accumulation and grain filling.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing seeds at 5 DAF; field-grown plants
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports grain filling and endosperm development via auxin/storage pathways
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mutation contributes to chalkiness and lower grain weight in double mutant context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of grain quality regulatory module, but perturbation risks severe chalkiness and yield penalty
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulator because interaction, reporter, EMSA and double-mutant analyses are central to the Results, though much mechanistic detail also relies on prior literature.
